message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="A11y fixes block Task 5 completion, not a follow-up">
Scott, quick correction: the team lead has already directed that these Task #5 a11y fixes must be completed BEFORE any Task #6 work resumes. This is not a follow-up -- it's a blocker on Task #5 completion. The team lead explicitly said: "Task 5 is NOT complete" and listed all 4 issues as must-fix before moving forward.

I've sent Luca the exact corrected template HTML for all three field groups. Once he implements, tests pass, and CI is green, I verify and Task #5 can be closed. Then Task #6 resumes.

On the `|safe` filter -- good catch noting it. Worth adding a comment near that usage in the handler so future developers know why it's safe and don't replicate the pattern with user input.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Another message from the earlier timeline. The a11y fixes Heydon is referring to were completed long ago -- Task 5 was closed and we're now past Task 20.

input.content:
Heydon -- this message appears to be from an earlier point in our project timeline. The a11y fixes you're referencing were completed, Task 5 was closed, and we've progressed through Task 20 (Edit todo title). HEAD is `203206b`, all 78 tests green, all tasks through #20 done.

If you have current a11y concerns about the codebase as it stands now, I'm happy to review. Otherwise I believe we're both idle waiting for new work from the team-lead.
